AT.name: Austria
AT.notes: 13% rate applies to certain cultural events, wine from farmers. 10% for food, books, passenger transport.

BE.name: Belgium
BE.notes: 6% for basic necessities, books, water. 12% for social housing, restaurant services, certain energy.

BG.name: Bulgaria
BG.notes: 9% for hotel accommodation, restaurant services (until 30 June 2025), books.

HR.name: Croatia
HR.notes: 13% for accommodation, food service. 5% for books, basic food, medical equipment.

CY.name: Cyprus
CY.notes: 9% for accommodation, restaurant services. 5% for books, newspapers, certain food.

CZ.name: Czech Republic
CZ.notes: As of 2024, two reduced rates (10% and 15%) merged into single 12% rate.

DK.name: Denmark
DK.notes: Denmark applies no reduced VAT rates. Zero rate for newspapers (digital and print).

EE.name: Estonia
EE.notes: Standard rate increased to 22% from 1 Jan 2024. 9% for accommodation, press. 13% for certain goods from 2025.

FI.name: Finland
FI.notes: Standard rate increased to 25.5% from 1 Sep 2024. 14% for food, restaurant services. 10% for books, pharma, transport.

FR.name: France
FR.notes: 10% for restaurants, transport, renovation. 5.5% for food, books, energy. 2.1% for press, certain medicines.

DE.name: Germany
DE.notes: 7% for food, books, newspapers, public transport, cultural events.

GR.name: Greece
GR.notes: 13% for food, energy, hotel. 6% for pharma, books, theatre. Reduced rates on certain islands.

HU.name: Hungary
HU.notes: Highest standard rate in EU. 18% for basic food, dairy, bakery. 5% for books, pharma, accommodation, new housing.

IE.name: Ireland
IE.notes: 13.5% for building services, energy. 9% for newspapers, e-publications, sport facilities. 4.8% for livestock.

IT.name: Italy
IT.notes: 10% for certain food, hotels, restaurants, renovation. 5% for certain food, social services. 4% for basic food, books, medical.

LV.name: Latvia
LV.notes: 12% for certain food, pharma, public transport, accommodation. 5% for fruit, vegetables, books.

LT.name: Lithuania
LT.notes: 9% for books, periodicals, accommodation, cultural/sport events. 5% for pharma, medical devices.

LU.name: Luxembourg
LU.notes: Lowest standard rate in EU. 8% for gas, electricity, certain services. 3% for food, books, children's clothing, water.

MT.name: Malta
MT.notes: 7% for accommodation. 5% for electricity, confectionery, printed matter, medical accessories.

NL.name: Netherlands
NL.notes: 9% for food, water, pharma, books, hotels, cultural events, passenger transport.

PL.name: Poland
PL.notes: 8% for pharma, transport, restaurants, hotels. 5% for food, books.

PT.name: Portugal
PT.notes: 13% for certain food, wine, agricultural inputs. 6% for basic food, books, pharma, transport.

RO.name: Romania
RO.notes: 9% for food, restaurants, accommodation, water. 5% for books, cultural events, social housing.

SK.name: Slovakia
SK.notes: As of 2025: standard rate 23% (was 20%). 19% for food, restaurants, accommodation, sport. 5% for books, pharma, basic food.

SI.name: Slovenia
SI.notes: 9.5% for food, accommodation, cultural events, transport. 5% for books, periodicals.

ES.name: Spain
ES.notes: 10% for food, water, transport, hotels, restaurants. 4% for bread, milk, books, pharma, medical devices.

SE.name: Sweden
SE.notes: 12% for food, restaurants, accommodation. 6% for books, newspapers, passenger transport, cultural events.

GB.name: United Kingdom
GB.notes: Post-Brexit: independent VAT regime. 5% for domestic energy, children's car seats. 0% for food, books, children's clothing, public transport.

NO.name: Norway
NO.notes: EEA member, not EU. 15% for food. 12% for transport, accommodation, cinema, broadcasting.

CH.name: Switzerland
CH.notes: Not EU/EEA. Rates updated 1 Jan 2024. 3.8% for accommodation. 2.6% for food, books, newspapers, medicine.

IS.name: Iceland
IS.notes: EEA member, not EU. 11% for food, accommodation, books, newspapers.
